L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a user-supplied HTTP request parameter is directly used in database operations without proper type enforcement or sanitization, allowing remote unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a user-supplied HTTP request parameter is directly used in database operations without proper type enforcement or sanitization, allowing remote unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

ground_truth.reason: The rowid parameter in the subscription info page is taken directly from unsanitized GET/POST input and used in database queries, allowing remote SQL injection.
